name: Chet Baker
abstract: Chesney Henry Baker Jr. (December 23, 1929 – May 13, 1988) was an American jazz trumpeter and vocalist.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: New Jazz at Carnegie
Midnight Performance: Thanksgiving Jazz at Carnegie Hall
Gerry Mulligan, Chet Baker and Stan Getz

works.t: Babes in Arms: My Funny Valentine
There Will Never Be Another You

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: All-Star Jam Session

works.t: The New Moon: Softly, as in a Morning Sunrise

about_quotes.speaker: Ingrid Sertso
about_quotes.evidence: I only met him once in Manhattan when he was already almost totally gone you know.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ingrid Sertso interviewed by Monk Rowe, Woodstock, New York, August 11, 2014

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Strazzeri
about_quotes.evidence: I played with him for years, Chet. About 30 years I played with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Strazzeri interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Drummond
about_quotes.evidence: I was even playing for instance Monday nights with Chet Baker. ... I used to go over to Chet’s mother’s house and we would rehearse. We had this Monday night gig in a pizza parlor in San Jose. And later Chet moved up to San Francisco and so he had me working.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Drummond interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 9, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Holman Bill
about_quotes.evidence: I did three dates with him out here, three albums... The James Dean Story. Chet Baker and Bud Shank, listed as the leaders on this one... I just made arrangements out of them for a medium-sized jazz band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Holman Bill — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Danko
about_quotes.evidence: I guess like the time with Chet, which was right after Woody Herman, was just the first time I played with him... my Chet Baker association, which went to about 1976 or so
about_quotes.doc_title: Harold Danko interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 28, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: In October 1955, I played a two days jazz festival in Lyon which featured Sydney Bechet, Chet Baker and myself.
